claim_candidate: A Transformer-based model (e.g., HART) robustly outperforms logistic regression on public sensor classification data across repeated seeds.
falsification_condition: If logistic regression's mean balanced accuracy is within 1% of Transformer's, or if the effect-to-noise ratio is <1.5, the hypothesis is falsified.
minimum_evidence_needed: One public dataset with 10 seeds, showing Transformer mean balanced accuracy > logistic regression by at least 2% and effect-to-noise ratio >2.
why_it_matters: HAR literature (arxiv_2209_11750_2022) shows Transformers outperform CNNs/LSTMs and are robust to sensor heterogeneity, suggesting a nonlinear advantage over logistic regression.
